The diagram shows the cardiac cycle. What is the duration of one cardiac cycle at 75 beats/minute?
questions.diagram_description: Graph of cardiac cycle showing pressure changes over time: atrial systole (0.1s), ventricular systole (0.3s), joint diastole (0.4s). Pressure curves for left atrium, left ventricle, and aorta shown with valve opening/closing events marked. Total cycle = 0.8 seconds

options.C: Rh- mother carries Rh+ fetus second time

questions.question: The diagram shows the structure of the human heart. Identify the valve between the left atrium and left ventricle.
questions.diagram_description: Frontal section of human heart showing: right atrium (RA), right ventricle (RV), left atrium (LA), left ventricle (LV), superior and inferior vena cava entering RA, pulmonary artery leaving RV, pulmonary veins entering LA, aorta leaving LV, tricuspid valve (between RA and RV), bicuspid/mitral valve (between LA and LV), pulmonary semilunar valve, aortic semilunar valve, chordae tendineae, papillary muscles

questions.question: Thrombocytopenia refers to:
The AV node delays impulse conduction to:

options.A: Allow ventricles to fill during atrial systole
options.B: Allow atria to relax before next beat

questions.question: Blood pressure is highest in:
The lymphatic system returns fluid to blood via:
The diagram shows the blood clotting cascade. Which factor is the final common pathway enzyme that converts fibrinogen to fibrin?
questions.diagram_description: Simplified clotting cascade: Intrinsic pathway (contact activation) and Extrinsic pathway (tissue factor) converging at Factor X activation. Factor Xa + Factor Va (prothrombinase complex) converts Prothrombin (Factor II) → Thrombin (Factor IIa). Thrombin converts Fibrinogen (Factor I) → Fibrin monomers → Factor XIIIa cross-links to stable fibrin clot

The diagram shows an ECG tracing. Which wave represents ventricular depolarization?
questions.diagram_description: ECG tracing showing: P wave (small, atrial depolarization), PR interval (conduction through AV node), QRS complex (ventricular depolarization – tall narrow deflections), ST segment (early ventricular repolarization), T wave (ventricular repolarization), with time axis (each small square = 0.04s) and voltage axis labeled

options.B: AV node to Purkinje fibres via bundle branches

questions.question: The diagram shows capillary exchange. What force drives fluid OUT of capillaries at the arterial end?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of capillary showing: arteriole end (hydrostatic pressure=35mmHg, oncotic pressure=25mmHg, net filtration=10mmHg), mid-capillary, venule end (hydrostatic pressure=15mmHg, oncotic pressure=25mmHg, net absorption=10mmHg). Protein (albumin) remaining in capillary creating oncotic pressure. Tissue fluid space around capillary. Lymph capillary collecting excess fluid

questions.question: Anaemia due to destruction of RBCs is called:

options.A: Right heart to lungs and back to left heart
options.B: Left heart to body and back to right heart

questions.question: Coronary arteries arise from:
The diagram shows blood groups. Which blood group has both anti-A and anti-B antibodies in plasma?
questions.diagram_description: Table/diagram showing ABO blood groups: Group A (antigen A on RBC, anti-B antibody in plasma), Group B (antigen B on RBC, anti-A antibody in plasma), Group AB (antigens A and B on RBC, no antibodies in plasma – universal recipient), Group O (no antigens on RBC, both anti-A and anti-B antibodies in plasma – universal donor)

options.A: Heart rate increases with venous return
options.B: Stroke volume increases with increased venous return (preload)
options.C: Blood pressure is proportional to heart size
options.D: Cardiac output is independent of filling pressure

The sinoatrial node fires at approximately how many times per minute intrinsically?

options.B: Percentage volume of RBCs in blood

questions.question: The diagram shows the structure of an artery, vein, and capillary. Which vessel has the thickest tunica media?
questions.diagram_description: Cross-section diagrams of three vessels: (A) Artery – thick tunica intima, very thick tunica media with smooth muscle and elastic fibres, tunica adventitia; (B) Vein – thin tunica media, valves present, large lumen; (C) Capillary – single layer of endothelium only, basement membrane, pericytes

questions.question: Fibrin clot is dissolved by:
Serum differs from plasma in that serum:
The diagram shows a normal ECG. What is the PR interval significance?
questions.diagram_description: ECG tracing with labeled intervals: P wave onset to QRS onset = PR interval (0.12-0.20s, represents time for impulse to travel from SA node through AV node to ventricular muscle). QRS duration = 0.06-0.10s. QT interval = time for ventricular depolarization and repolarization. Each annotation with normal values shown

options.C: Conduction time from SA node to ventricles through AV node

questions.question: Sickle cell anaemia is caused by:

options.B: Point mutation causing substitution of glutamic acid by valine in beta globin

questions.question: Valves are present in veins to:
Which vitamin is essential for synthesis of clotting factors II, VII, IX, X?
The coronary sinus drains into:
The diagram shows lymphatic capillaries. What is the unique feature of lymph capillaries compared to blood capillaries?
questions.diagram_description: Comparison diagram: Blood capillary (closed tube, tight junctions between endothelial cells, continuous basement membrane, two-way exchange). Lymph capillary (blind-ended, overlapping endothelial cells acting as one-way valves, discontinuous basement membrane, only absorbs fluid from tissues, larger lumen, valves present in larger lymphatics)

options.B: They are blind-ended with one-way flap valves allowing easy entry of tissue fluid
options.D: They are present in all tissues including cartilage

questions.question: Polycythaemia is a condition characterized by:

options.B: Ischemia of cardiac muscle due to reduced coronary blood flow
